name: Ribera Alta Loop
name_original: La Ribera Alta del Xúquer — Circular desde Carlet
description: Cycle 41.2 miles (66.4 km) on this moderate road route through Valencia's quiet interior, featuring river valleys and orange groves.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Carlet, located in the La Ribera Alta region of Valencia, Spain, offer diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area features rural scenery and varied terrain, including sections with climbs, as evidenced by its inclusion in the "Volta a la Comunitat Valenciana" professional race. Roads are generally well-maintained, providing suitable conditions for both competitive and recreational road cycling. The region's natural features provide an appealing backdrop for active exploration. Best road cycling routes around Carlet The most popular road cycling route is The Upper Ribera of Xúquer — Circular from Carlet, a 41.2 miles (66.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route guides cyclists through the scenic Upper Ribera of Xúquer region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Júcar-Turia Transfer loop from Benimodo, a moderate 21.8 miles (35.2 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and views of the Júcar and Turia river areas. Local road cyclists also love the Tous Reservoir – Júcar-Turia Transfer loop from Benimodo, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through the area around Tous Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Carlet is defined by rural scenery, varied Valencian terrain, and sections with climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging routes. The routes in Carlet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2,200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carlet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Carlet?
A: There are over 10 dedicated road cycling routes around Carlet, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Carlet?
A: Road cycling routes in Carlet feature diverse landscapes, ranging from rural scenery to varied Valencian terrain. You can expect sections with climbs, as the area has even been part of the professional 'Volta a la Comunitat Valenciana' race, indicating well-maintained roads and challenging gradients.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Carlet offers routes for various ability levels. While most routes are classified as moderate, there are at least two easier options available, providing a good starting point for less experienced road cyclists.

Q: What are some scenic points or landmarks I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the La Ribera Alta region. You might encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvador Castell Park&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2593714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Estepar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1504170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tous Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3314408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samaruc Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Carlet?
A: Yes, Carlet is a great starting point for circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e857216867/the-upper-ribera-of-xuquer-circular-from-carlet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Upper Ribera of Xúquer — Circular from Carlet&lt;/a&gt;, a 66.4 km trail that guides cyclists through the scenic Upper Ribera of Xúquer region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Carlet?
A: The Valencia region, including Carlet, generally enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for cycling much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential cooler, wetter conditions in winter.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there is at least one difficult-rated route. Additionally, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar to Turia Canal – Weir of the Magre River loop from Carlet&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 97 km with over 800 meters of elevation gain, offer a substantial workout.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by reservoirs or bodies of water?
A: Absolutely. The area around Carlet features routes that include water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40020270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tous Reservoir – Júcar-Turia Transfer loop from Benimodo&lt;/a&gt; leads through the area around Tous Reservoir, offering picturesque views.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Carlet?
A: The road cycling routes in Carlet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, rural scenery, and the well-maintained roads that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any longer distance road cycling options from Carlet?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar to Turia Canal – Weir of the Magre River loop from Carlet&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 97 kilometers, providing an excellent option for a full day out on the bike.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Júcar and Turia river areas?
A: Yes, you can explore the river areas on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar-Turia Transfer loop from Benimodo&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 35.2 km path offers varied terrain and views of both the Júcar and Turia river regions.
tips: On the side of the road is the weir over the Riu Magre, one of the most attractive points on the road from Tavernes to Lliria. With some time you can see different birds

faqs.question: How difficult is The Upper Ribera of Xúquer — Circular from Carlet road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered moderate, but don't let that fool you. While the initial climbs are smooth, they can be intense, making it a challenging ride for its length. It features significant elevation changes over its 66.4 km distance, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this 66.4 km route in about 3 hours and 9 minutes. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Upper Ribera of Xúquer from Carlet?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be hot, so if you ride then, it's best to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, one notable point of interest is the Weir of the Magre River. The route also takes you through beautiful scenery typical of inland Valencia, including quiet roads, lush river valleys, and agricultural areas like orange groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Carlet circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Carlet, making it convenient to find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas in Carlet, especially near the town center, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while there aren't specific restrictions against dogs, it's generally not suitable for cycling with pets due to traffic and the nature of road biking. If you're looking for dog-friendly options, consider trails designed for walking or mountain biking where dogs are explicitly permitted.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths in the Valencian Community.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows well-maintained roads, reported to be in perfect condition. You'll experience a mix of smooth but intense climbs and descents, traversing diverse landscapes from agricultural plains and orange groves to areas closer to the Júcar River.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily its own distinct route, it does briefly intersect with sections of other paths. You might encounter small portions of routes like Cicloruta del Campillo hasta la Garrofera, Monserrat - Montroy, and Camí Mistera 3.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and abundant nature, guiding you through landscapes typical of inland Valencia. The route features very quiet roads with low traffic, lush river valleys, and agricultural areas, including extensive orange groves, especially as you approach the Júcar River.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself passes through quiet, low-traffic areas, you will find amenities in the towns you pass through or near the start/end point in Carlet. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, but you can plan stops in villages for refreshments.
